component_identification.content: Base Telestrations 8-player set includes 8 erasable sketch books, 8 dry-erase markers, 8 cleaning cloths, 1 die (numbered categories), 1,700+ word prompts on cards (or equivalent large prompt deck by edition), timer/sand timer in some printings, and rules. Party Pack editions raise player capacity with more books/markers.

core_game_loop.content: Turn rhythm: active side reveals or frames information, teammates/opponents respond under that game’s communication limits, then resolve points or survival state and rotate responsibility. Teach this as a repeating three-step cycle: present signal, interpret signal, then resolve consequences. New players should always know whose decision matters right now and what hidden information cannot be shared. Most confusion comes from breaking communication restrictions or skipping reveal order, so keep sequence strict.

detailed_rules.content: Give each player a sketch book, marker, cloth, and a secret word (often from card + die roll/category). Round sequence alternates write→draw→guess repeatedly: first player writes word, passes book; next player draws that word, passes; next player writes guess of drawing, and so on until books return to owners. No letters/numbers in drawings unless your edition variant permits symbols minimally. After return, each player reveals book progression for laughs and scoring. Scoring variants differ: many groups use points for correct final guesses and for matching original word; some play purely for humor. Keep pass direction constant and wait for timer each step. If player cannot draw, stick figures are valid; clarity beats art quality.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Many groups use optional points: one point for correct guess links and/or for original word surviving to end; exact method by edition card. Officially it is often a laugh-focused party experience where winner is optional. If scoring, highest total after agreed rounds wins.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Prioritize iconic visual elements over artistic detail. 2) Guessers should write the clearest literal interpretation, not joke answers, if playing to score. 3) Keep books moving in rhythm; long delays reduce reveal energy. 4) Reuse recurring symbols for fast communication in multi-round sessions. 5) If teaching new players, do a short demo chain first.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 426; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
